page: Microsoft Azure GS5-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the GS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 x86_64 processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The server is equipped with a 40 MB L3 cache, 448.0 GB of system memory providing 28.0 GB per core, and 918 GB of local storage. There are no GPUs bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core CPU performance is weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% and bottom 25% respectively. However, multi-core performance is average, as shown by the stress-ng multi-core score of 11275.71 and Geekbench multi-core score of 7480.0. Memory bandwidth is consistently average, reaching 71.87 GB/sec for large block read-only operations. Database operations and Redis SET performance also rank in the middle 50%, with Redis handling 19.34 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the GS5-16 is its low single-threaded processing speed, which limits its effectiveness for single-core workloads or real-time text generation in LLM inference, where text generation speeds are poor. Qualitatively, the server offers high resource density in terms of memory capacity relative to compute cores, making it a cost-efficient choice for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-intensive tasks. It is best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that benefit from its 448.0 GB RAM allocation and local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure GS5-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 x86_64 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 448.0 GB of RAM, 918 GB of local storage, and no GPU. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core capabilities but average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, with Redis SET operations reaching 19.34 million operations per second. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 28.0 GB per core makes this server qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications. It is highly suited for large databases, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS5-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 448.0 GB of RAM, and 918 GB of local storage, designed for large databases and in-memory caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure GS5-16 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 448.0 GB of RAM, and 918 GB of local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM capacity and local storage for database and in-memory caching workloads.
